items.title: Safety officers to patrol Hobart CBD to curb antisocial behaviour

children.content: A trio of council-employed public safety monitors will patrol "hot spots" in the Hobart CBD, to try to curb antisocial behaviour and theft.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: Travelling on the Spirit of Tasmania ferries is about to become more expensive

children.content: Customers booking a Bass Strait crossing from tomorrow will be hit with a 15 per cent surcharge. The government says it's to help ferry operator TT-line deal with rising fuel costs.

image.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart, with a silhouette of a large building in front of it.

items.title: Meet the teenager with the cricketing world at her feet

children.content: Mia Barwick is just 15 and a multi-sport prodigy, bound for cricketing glory. She's part of the next generation of female sporting stars — but with that comes pressure of the spotlight.

image.altText: Cricketer Mia Barwick at pathway training 2026-02-10 11:02:00

items.title: Tristan Gourlay breaks Australian sailing record for fastest global circumnavigation

children.content: Tristan Gourlay has broken the Australian record for the fastest solo, unassisted circumnavigation of the world — completing it in 138 days. The record he broke was set by his father, Ken Gourlay, in 2007.

items.title: Commuters say buses 'absolutely packed' as free transport starts

children.content: Some commuters in Hobart say the buses have been busier than usual, as free public transport rolls out across Tasmania.

image.altText: People walk off a Metro Tas bus in Hobart's CBD.

items.title: A minute before Trump's wild Iran claim, oil traders made 'abnormal' moves

children.content: Global stock markets, which for the past month have been remarkably restrained as the Middle East crisis unfolded, have begun to wobble.

articles.title: Israel backs down over holy site, Pope rebukes Hegseth, and what else you should know

children.content: Donald Trump renews threats against Iran's energy infrastructure while his secretary of war faces an apparent rebuke from the pope.

image.altText: A man wearing a helmet and flak jacket with the word PRESS on it looks at the camera with building rubble behind him

articles.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Kharg Island if Iran refuses deal to end war

children.content: As thousands more US troops arrive in the Middle East, Donald Trump threatens to wipe out Iran's energy plants and oil wells if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: Donald Trump standing in a doorway aboard Air Force One.

articles.title: Israel passes death penalty for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks

children.content: The law, long-sought by Israel's far-right politicians, makes death by hanging the default punishment for West Bank Palestinians convicted of killing Israelis.

image.altText: Benjamin Netanyahu looking stern at a press conference.

gallery.altText: A satellite view of the Strait of Hormuz.

thumbnail.altText: A satellite view of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Spain closes airspace to US planes involved in Middle East war

children.content: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt says US President Donald Trump is interested in getting Arab countries to pay for the war in the Middle East. Follow live.

image.altText: The front of  a grey plane with the word U.S Air Force painted in black paint.

items.title: We are 'reshaping the Middle East', Israeli ambassador tells Press Club

children.content: Israeli ambassador Hillel Newman gives an address at the National Press Club.
Follow live.

image.altText: a male politician wearing a suit and a pink tie

items.title: The day Porepunkah went quiet and learned Dezi Freeman was dead

children.content: Seven months after a gunman killed two police officers on a nearby property, the tiny town woke to news Dezi Freeman was dead.

articles.title: From Porepunkah to Thologolong: The long search for Dezi Freeman

children.content: When the parents of Senior Constable Vadim de Waart-Hottart flew to Melbourne for a police service, little did they know their son's killer would emerge just days later.

image.altText: An image showing a middle-aged man staring down the barrel of the camera.

articles.title: Police continue hunt for anyone who assisted fugitive Dezi Freeman

children.content: Police say they will come down hard on anyone who helped Dezi Freeman, who was shot dead by police yesterday after a massive seven-month manhunt.

image.altText: A screen grab of a man looking seriously at a the camera.

items.title: Iran's 'safe passage' signals a new global divide

children.content: China is now the world's largest crude oil importer, and Iran — though a discounted and often opaque supplier — remains a consistent part of that supply mix.

image.altText: China President Xi Jinping.

items.title: How one man's stolen identity was used to run a cult

children.content: In mid-2009 British citizen Simon Kadwill-Kelly was catching a ferry returning from France. Within minutes of trying to board he found out his identity had been stolen.

image.altText: A man dressed in large ears and a Pinoccio nose with a police hat.

items.title: 'I'm sick of it': One in two Australians priced out of healthcare system

children.content: One in two Australians missed out on health care they needed last year mainly because they could not afford it, according to a new report by the peak healthcare consumers group.

articles.title: It's $2.5m a dose and it's giving some children another chance at life

children.content: Children born with a rare neuromuscular disorder are being given another chance in life thanks to a new treatment experts are calling a "game-changer".

image.altText: A young child being held up by her father

articles.title: Vaping likely to cause cancer, new Australian review of evidence finds

children.content: Nicotine-based vapes are likely to cause lung and oral cancer, according to a new review of evidence. Experts say there needs to be tougher enforcement of vape laws in Australia.

image.altText: young person with smoke coming out of their mouth after vaping

items.title: Céline Dion to perform again after years-long health-related hiatus

children.content: The My Heart Will Go On singer disclosed in 2022 that she had a rare neurological disease, indefinitely cancelling her shows.

image.altText: Celine Dion in a gold dress on stage, singing into a microphone with her eyes closed and one hand in the air

items.title: How to tell if you have insufficient insurance for your home and contents

children.content: If your home is underinsured, it means your insurance policy won't fully cover the cost of rebuilding, repairing or replacing it.

items.title: Transphobia nearly killed Kaz. Now he just wants to be 'me'

children.content: This WA teenager was depressed, ill and missing out on school. He says it was part of the toll transphobia took on him.

image.altText: A boy stands in front of ocean scenery.

items.title: Moon mission quiz: How far does your lunar knowledge reach?

children.content: As NASA gets ready to launch humans around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years, buckle up and let's see if your space knowledge is up to our quiz.

image.altText: Image of half a Moon against a black background.

items.title: Five social media companies investigated over response to teen ban

children.content: Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are being investigated for potential noncompliance with the ban, almost four months after the laws came into force.

items.title: Passengers fear further V/Line overcrowding as free travel begins

children.content: Commuters say they are worried overcrowding on V/Line train services could worsen as free travel begins for Victorians to ease the pain of the fuel crisis.

image.altText: The interior of a full train carriage showing full seats and the aisle full of standing passengers

items.title: Businesses report increase in abuse from customers as fuel crisis worsens

children.content: From pile-on comments on social media to roadside slurs, local businesses are reminding their communities to "be kind to the little guy" amid the fuel crisis.

image.altText: a woman stares at the camera with a houseboat in the background

items.title: Supermarket chicken could get cheaper due to grain breakthrough

children.content: Australian researchers develop a high-protein sorghum variety, hoping to replace expensive livestock feed with systems that could lead to cheaper meat and eggs.

items.title: Consumer confidence hits 53-year low, ASX and AUD up

children.content: Consumer confidence across Australia has hit its worst level since tracking began in 1973, according to the latest ANZ-Roy Morgan survey, but the ASX 200 has bounced back to rise by almost 1 per cent. Follow the day's events on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Black-robed lawyers and 'killer' beef at Kimberley court

children.content: It was a court hearing unlike any other, complete with country music, "killer" beef, and gaggles of kids and dogs dashing between the black-clad lawyers.

image.altText: A man with a guitar surrounded by children

items.title: Draft report into ANU's controversial cuts critical of university's council

children.content: ANU's council approved a massive $250 million cost-cutting program without clear evidence it was needed or achievable, according to an assessment in a draft audit office report.

items.title: 'You are just an immigrant': Millions in unpaid wages, modern slavery uncovered

children.content: Millions in stolen wages, threats of visa cancellation and modern slavery by Australian employers have been uncovered in the first year of a national program.

image.altText: a woman from colombia who now lives in australia smiles at the camera

items.title: Concerns about light and noise from $1b outback energy project resolved

children.content: The battery project, which will provide back-up power for up to 80,000 homes in Broken Hill, will go ahead with new conditions after a dispute was resolved between the company and an outback stargazing business.

image.altText: Aerial view of highway with a few residences pictured on the ground.

items.title: Wine manufacturer De Bortoli has its own solution for workers amidst housing crisis

children.content: There is plenty of work on offer in the western Riverina, but finding somewhere to live can be a challenge. The situation is forcing some employers to think outside the box.

gallery.altText: A man stands between rows of a vineyard.

items.title: Two states have made free public transport free. Here's when it starts

children.content: As fuel prices continue to rise some states have moved to make public transport free, while others say that fares are already low enough. Find out where.

items.title: Myanmar's military chief makes move to become next president

children.content: Myanmar's military chief has stepped down to seek the presidency in the general's latest efforts to maintain power behind the facade of a civilian administration.

image.altText: Commander-in-Chief of Myanmar's armed forces, Senior General Min Aung Hlaing

children.content: Warren Purse, a Territory paramedic with over 20 years' experience, is assaulted on the job about three or four times a year.

items.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent

items.title: Banned Xerri reveals Essendon abuse leading to blood-wiping incident
